item.title: 5/16: The Uplift
item.description: David Begnaud meets a man who has attended the Kentucky Derby for 79 years in a row – and his dying wish to make it there one last time.

item.title: FBI sought to interview top election official in Wisconsin's Milwaukee County
item.description: The FBI attempted to interview the director of elections in Milwaukee County, Wisconsin, the county clerk's office said in a statement.

item.title: $100,000 short-term CD vs. $100,000 high-yield savings account: Which will earn more this year?
item.description: The interest-earning potential of a short-term CD and a high-yield savings account is similar now, but not identical.

item.title: Trump and Xi agree Strait of Hormuz "must remain open"
item.description: President Trump and China's Xi Jinping met in Beijing on Thursday, while Israel and Lebanon resumed peace talks in Washington.

item.title: Small business optimism impacted by global events
item.description: New small-business data raises concerns about the impact of certain economic conditions and global events. CBS News' Jill Schlesinger has more. (Sponsored by AT&T Business)

item.title: Few migrants held at Guantanamo, despite Trump's pledge
item.description: A CBS News review of internal government documents and information provided to Congress shows immigration detention facilities at Guantanamo Bay are nearly empty.

item.title: Pilot describes landing small plane in Atlantic Ocean: "My first thought was, 'We didn't die'"
item.description: A small plane pilot is being credited with saving lives after an emergency landing in the Atlantic Ocean that prompted a rescue by the U.S. Air Force. Pilot Ian Nixon describes what happened and how everyone on the plane survived.

item.title: Janet Walsh murder: Will new clue solve 1979 cold case?
item.description: The murder of a young Pennsylvania woman remains unsolved for 34 years - can a determined detective and new technology bring her killer to justice?

item.title: Ukrainians buried under rubble after deadly Russian strike
item.description: Rescue efforts continue in Kyiv, Ukraine, after two days of deadly Russian strikes. CBS News' Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: Tense Taiwan warnings at Trump-Xi meeting
item.description: Chinese state media summaries are highlighting apparent comments about Taiwan during the Trump-Xi meeting. CBS News' Anna Coren and Ramy Inocencio report.

item.title: Spoiler alert! "Survivor" contestants talk being voted off after another double elimination
item.description: (Spoilers ahead) "CBS Mornings" reveals the castaways voted off during Wednesday's episode of "Survivor 50" in another double elimination. They discuss being part of the franchise and their legacies in the game.

item.title: U.K. health secretary resigns in scathing letter, setting up leadership fight
item.description: Health Secretary Wes Streeting quit embattled British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet in what is expected to be a precursor to challenging his leadership.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Sailors from doomed 1845 polar expedition identified through DNA
item.description: The identification of the remains also resolved a decades-long debate about the worst disaster in the history of British polar exploration.

item.title: National Recording Registry adds Taylor Swift, Beyoncé, Ray Charles, more
item.description: The Library of Congress revealed this year's list of 25 recordings to be preserved for future generations on the National Recording Registry.

item.title: Huge twist in Alex Murdaugh case after court overturns murder conviction
item.description: Alex Murdaugh, the former South Carolina lawyer who was convicted of murder, will get a new trial and have his convictions overturned, the state's Supreme Court ruled Wednesday. CBS News' Eva Pilgrim reports.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that might just become essentials in your everyday life. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Janet Walsh murder: Will new clue solve 1979 cold case?
item.description: The murder of a young Pennsylvania woman remains unsolved for 34 years - can a determined detective and new technology bring her killer to justice?

item.title: Businesses starting to receive their IEEPA tariff refunds
item.description: "I'd been checking the status feverishly to see if anything was in my bank account," one small business owner said.

item.title: NASA spacecraft to fly past Mars on voyage to rare metal asteroid
item.description: NASA's Psyche spacecraft will slingshot past Mars on Friday, on its way toward a rare metal-rich asteroid.

item.title: China's Xi warns Trump about "conflicts" if Taiwan isn't "handled properly"
item.description: President Trump is in Beijing meeting with China's President Xi Jinping, with the two leaders aiming to stabilize their trading relationship after last year's trade war.

item.title: Behind the rise of fast food and how it shaped the U.S.
item.description: In the series "USA to Z," "CBS Mornings" goes behind the scenes for a taste of history and shows how fast food has become part of American culture.

item.title: Latest news on Trump-Xi summit as Taiwan becomes key issue discussed
item.description: President Trump's first full day in China included a meeting with President Xi Jinping, where Taiwan became a crucial topic, according to Chinese state media. CBS News' Weijia Jiang, Ramy Inocencio and Tony Dokoupil report.

item.title: Hero pilot recounts crash landing in Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Ian Nixon, a veteran pilot from the Bahamas, put the plane he was flying down in the ocean without anyone suffering serious injuries.

item.title: Rare blue-green diamond sells for record $17.3 million at auction
item.description: The 5.5-carat "Ocean Dream" was found in Central Africa in the 1990s.

item.title: Workers are getting paid to teach AI how to do their jobs
item.description: AI companies are recruiting a wide range of temp workers, from writers to wine enthusiasts, for hourly-paid gigs to help train their language models.

item.title: Trump demands Medicaid data for deportation. Some states go further.
item.description: Several states have required their health agencies to take on another job: verifying immigration status among Medicaid recipients and reporting them to the U.S. Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: House leaders to take action on process for sexual misconduct claims
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson and Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries are launching a bipartisan task force aimed at addressing how sexual misconduct claims are handled within the House of Representatives, multiple sources confirm to CBS News.

item.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer rejects mounting calls to resign
item.description: Despite a fifth of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's fellow Labour Party lawmakers calling on him to step down, he says he'll "get on with governing."

item.title: 34 years later, detective closes haunting cold case
item.description: Andy Gall investigated the 1979 murder of Janet Walsh -- and more than 30 years later followed through on his promise to her family to bring justice
